Title: DA10--National Teleradiology Program Picture Archive and Communication System Upgrade (VA-26-00042676)
Awardee: INTELERAD MEDICAL SYSTEMS INC MONTREAL H2L 4L8
Description: This contract action is for a sole source, firm-fixed-price contract with Intelerad Medical Systems, Inc. (Intelerad), 800 De Maisonneuve Blvd East, 14th Floor, Montreal, Quebec. for the upgrade of the existing National Teleradiology Program (NTP) Picture Archive and Communication System (PACS) from IntelePACS version 4.9 to version 5.7 or the most current version available at the time of implementation, including migration of all NTPspecific applications. The contract will also provide ensuing system maintenance and technical support for the upgraded platform. The Statutory Authority for this effort falls under Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Overhaul VA Deviation 6.103-1 entitled, Only One Responsible Source and No Other Supplies or Services Will Satisfy Agency Requirements. See attached redacted J&A. The Point of Contact for this action is: Sean McAvoy, Contract Specialist; e-mail: Sean.McAvoy1@va.gov. The North American Industry Classification Systems (NAICS) code for this action is 541511. The Product Service Code for this action is DA10.

Title: Z2BZ--Base Elizabeth City Building 48 Female Restroom Rehabilitation
Awardee: G5 ENTERPRISES, LLC Norfolk VA 23510 USA
Description: This award was fixed-price and competitive. Rationale of award: Solicitation Provision 52.212-2 stated that the Government will make an award to the quoter who provides the lowest priced technically acceptable quote. Pursuant to 52.212-2, quotations were evaluated in an efficient and minimally burdensome fashion: The lowest priced quote was evaluated on technical acceptability and if it was found to be acceptable, an award would have been made. When it was found to be unacceptable, the next lowest price was evaluated. This process continued until a technically acceptable offer was identified. G5 Enterprises submitted the lowest priced technically acceptable quote. They were the lowest priced quoter who provided all of the required preaward submittals listed in 10.b. of the SOW, which were necessary to determine technical acceptability.

Description: THIS IS NOT A SOLICITATION This is a CONTRACT AWARD NOTICE. The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers - St. Paul District required 10,000 gallons of #2 Diesel (dyed) for Off-Road Use, delivered to the USACE Crane Barge Wade and attending equipment, at locations to be determined (TBD) along the Upper Mississippi River between Lock & Dam 7 (La Crescent, MN) and Lock & Dam 10 (Guttenberg, IA). No Biodiesel or Biodiesel blends will be accepted. Deliveries will be in quantities of between 1,000 and 4,000 gallons per delivery as requested by the On-Site Supervisor, beginning after April 20 and continuing for up to six weeks. Delivery will be by straight truck with fuel nozzle type fill capabilities. The USACE fuel barge will be manned and controlled by two Government employees during transfer. Requirement was informally solicited (i.e. oral quotes) amongst known fuel vendors using Simplified Acquisition Procedures. If vendors wish to be included in notifications for future requirements of this type (similar quantities of fuel in this area), please contact the POC listed below by email.

Title: M776 155mm Cannon Tubes
Awardee: BAE SYSTEMS LAND & ARMAMENTS L.P. Louisville KY 40214 USA
Description: On April 1, 2026, contract W15QKN-26-D-0006 was awarded to BAE Systems Land & Armaments, L.P. under the authority of Class Deviation 2024-O0005, Temporary Authorizations for Covered Contracts Related to Ukraine, Taiwan, and Israel, for the production and delivery of the M776 Cannon Tubes to support the M777A2 Howitzer. The award of this contract intends to meet the Government's concurrent requirements to replenish the United States (U.S.) Army ammunition stockpiles, given their rapid depletion amid ongoing armed conflicts; and to establish a second domestic source for M776 Cannon Tubes, both at the lowest possible schedule and performance risks, which a competitive action would jeopardize. BAE Systems Land & Armaments, L.P. is the only existing non-government domestic source with the requisite technology and capability to meet the Government's requirements.

Title: USS SAMPSON (DDG 102) FY26 Depot Modernization Period (DMP)
Awardee: VIGOR MARINE LLC Portland OR 97217 USA
Description: Vigor Marine LLC, Portland, Oregon was awarded a $252,849,606 firm-fixed-price (FFP) contract action for maintenance, modernization, and repair of USS SAMPSON (DDG 102) Fiscal 2026 Depot Modernization Period. The scope of this acquisition includes all labor, supervision, equipment, production, testing, facilities, and quality assurance necessary to prepare for and accomplish the Chief of Naval Operations (CNO) Availability for critical modernization, maintenance, and repair programs. This contract includes options, which, if exercised, would bring the cumulative value of this contract to $253,195,124. This contract was not competitively procured, but in accordance with 10 U.S. Code 3204(a)(3)(Industrial Mobilization).

Title: Starlink Mini Terminals and Accessories
Awardee: RED ORANGE NORTH AMERICA INC. Fort Washington PA 19034 USA
Description: A. The Contractor shall furnish and deliver items listed below to the U.S. Embassy Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia in accordance with the specifications and terms and conditions set forth herein. The materials and equipment requested are meant to provide medical clinics in Omnogobi, Dornogobi, and Dundgovi Province, Mongolia with Starlink Mini Terminals needed to provide internet connectivity to ambulances during medical emergencies and increase standard of medical care in a prehospital setting. B. This is a firm-fixed price type of purchase order/contract. C. The prices listed below shall include all labor, materials, overhead, profit, and transportation necessary to deliver the required items to the U.S. Embassy Ulaanbaatar, Denver Street #3, 11th Micro District, Ulaanbaatar 14190, Mongolia. D. The Contractor shall be responsible for managing delivery, safely and concisely packaging of all items to the address mentioned above, paragraph C, by 16 May 2026. The Contractor shall maintain communication with the Authorized Government Representative (AGR) for prompt delivery. E. Upon receipt of all items, the AGR will inspect, and inventory all procured items. All Starlink Terminals and accessories shall be ready for inventory on/about 16 May 2026. After inspection, if the AGR deems any of the item(s) to be unacceptable, the Contractor shall be responsible for replacing the item(s) within a reasonable amount of time from notification as determined by the AGR. The replacement item(s) shall meet the requirements as stated in the SOW. Once all items have been inventoried and inspected by the AGR, the AGR will take custody of the items until such a time they can be distributed to Medical Providers in the Gobi region.
